s: Prompt engineering optimized one string for one call. Agents broke that frame: across a fifty-step loop, the q
b: Prompt engineering optimized one string for one call. Agents broke that frame: across a fifty-step loop, the question is no longer how to phrase a request but what state — instructions, tools, memory, retrieved evidence,
Every call an agent makes is assembled from the same six components, and they all draw on one account. The system prompt establishes identity and inva

When the corpus is much larger than the window, there is no debate. A 10-million-document knowledge base at ~500 tokens each is 5B tokens against a 20
Everything in the window dies when the session ends. Memory is the set of structures that survive — and agents use three tiers, distinguished by scope
A long-running agent will hit the budget no matter how disciplined the curation. Compaction is the standard escape: when fill crosses a threshold (typ

s: A tool is the API between intelligence and the world — and the model never sees your code, only its surface .
b: A tool is the API between intelligence and the world — and the model never sees your code, only its surface . The name, the description, and the parameter schema are the model's entire understanding of what the tool does
When you give a model a tool, you hand it a contract written in three fields: a name , a description , and a parameter schema (JSON Schema, almost alw
Most bad agent behavior traces to bad tools, not bad models. The rules below are the ones with the highest return; they are deliberately few, because
Half of tool design is the call; the other half is the return, and it is the half people skip. Whatever a tool gives back is injected verbatim into th
Every agent host wants to connect to every data source and service. Before a standard existed, each pairing was a bespoke integration: your agent fram

b: A capable model wired straight into a shell is not a product; it is an incident report with a future timestamp. The harness — sandbox, permissions, verification, recovery, human gates — is everything around the model tha
By 2026 the strange fact of the agent market is that competitors often run the same frontier models and ship wildly different products. The difference
The sandbox is where EQ A4.1's second factor gets physically enforced. The design stance is borrowed from security engineering, not from trust: assume
Inside the sandbox, the permission layer decides per-action: allow , ask , or deny . Two principles carry most of the design. First, allowlist, don't
The single highest-leverage component of a harness is the verifier, because of an asymmetry covered in Vol II · §5.7: for code, math, and structured t
Verification tells you an attempt failed; checkpoints make that information affordable. The agent-native unit of recovery is the commit : cheap (milli
Where exactly does a human belong in the loop? The principled answer falls out of §4.5: gate by undo cost, not by anxiety. If an action is cheaply rev

s: An agent is a loop, and a loop is a product of probabilities: fifty steps at 99% each is a coin flip. Models i
b: An agent is a loop, and a loop is a product of probabilities: fifty steps at 99% each is a coin flip. Models improve on someone else's schedule; the loop is yours to engineer today. This chapter is about designing the it
Every agent demo that dazzles in five steps and dies in fifty is the same chart. A task that takes \(n\) sequential steps, each succeeding with probab
The standard answer to flaky steps is retries, and with one crucial precondition it works spectacularly. If a failed attempt can be detected and retri
Retries fix steps that fail loudly. The more expensive pathology is the loop that never fails at all — it just stops going anywhere. Every production
Sections 5.1–5.3 assemble into one structure, and nearly every serious agent system converges on it independently: plan the next move, act on the smal
Multi-agent is not a virtue; it is a topology decision, and the null hypothesis — one agent, one loop — wins more often than the conference talks sugg
Past a few hundred steps, the enemy stops being step failure and becomes state amnesia : the context window fills, compaction or restarts shed detail,
Once the loop is reliable, it is usually overpaying: a verified retry loop happily runs the flagship model on steps a model a tenth the price handles

s: Five chapters of this volume taught you to build agents. This one teaches you to know whether they work, why t
b: Five chapters of this volume taught you to build agents. This one teaches you to know whether they work, why they fail, and what they cost — because in production, you ship what you measure, and an unmeasured agent is an
Classical LLM evaluation assumes a clean contract: one input, one output, one reference to compare against. Agents break every clause of that contract
Like the testing pyramid in software, agent evals trade fidelity against speed. Cheap, deterministic checks run thousands of times a day and catch mos
pass@k answers: if I let the agent attempt the task \(k\) times, what is the probability that at least one attempt succeeds? Estimating it naively is
